Barack Obama:
Barack Hussein Obama: Biography

Barack Hussein Obama (birth: 4 August, 1961) is an American politician and was the 44th President of the United States. As the first African American president he left a huge impression in the minds of the world. His life is memorable as a history of social and political struggle.

Early life

Obama was born in Honolulu. He obtained a Bachelor of Arts degree in political science from Columbia University in 1983. Later he worked as a community organizer in Chicago, where a deep understanding about his social resources and social relations had developed.

Education and political beginning

In 1988 Obama was admitted to Harvard Law School, where he became known as the first black president of the Harvard Law Review. From the time of his education he expressed deep interest toward social justice and social reform.

Political career

From 1997 to 2004 Obama performed responsibility as senator of the state of Illinois. Later from 2005 to 2008 he worked as the United States senator representing Illinois. In his senate career he had contributed to huge reforms related to health, education and social policy.

As president

In 2008 Obama was elected president as the candidate of the Democratic Party and performed responsibility from 2009 to 2017. In his first administration he made huge efforts for distribution of social resources, health projects and increasing national unity. In his first administration huge projects related to social justice and distribution of social resources were launched.

Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping: Biography

Birth and Early Life
Xi Jinping (born 15 June 1953) is a Chinese politician and the fifth highest leader of the People's Republic of China. He serves as the general secretary of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) and chairman of the state’s Central Military Commission, for which he has been serving as CCP general secretary since 2012 and as president of China since 2013. His father Xi Zhongxun was a high-ranking communist leader who was politically removed during the Cultural Revolution. After this event, Xi Jinping was sent as a teenager to Liangjiahe village in Shaanxi province, where he lived in a yaodong (which was used as a residence).

Early Career and Political Rise
Xi Jinping maintained a low profile at the beginning of his career, although he gained social and political understanding from his experience of living in the village during the Cultural Revolution. He joined the CCP after multiple failed attempts and served as a local party secretary. During this time, he worked to build social connections and participated in local party activities.

Political Rise and Leadership
Xi Jinping began his political career from local CCP activities and in later years achieved higher positions. In 2012, he was elected as CCP general secretary and then served as chairman of the CMC. In 2013, he was elected as president of China and as chairman of the state Central Military Commission
Dilma Vana Rousseff:
Dilma Vana Rousseff (birth: 14 December, 1947) is a famous economist and politician of Brazil. She is known as the first female President in the history of Brazil, who served from 2011 to 2016. Her impeachment and removal happened on 31 August, 2016.
Rousseff was born in a upper middle class family in Belo Horizonte. In her youth she became a socialist and participated in politics. She served in the cabinet of Brazil’s first President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva—first as Minister of Mines and Energy (2003-2005) and later as Chief of Staff (2005-2010).
In 2011 she won the presidential election and became the first female President in the history of Brazil. In her administration, in the field of economy and social development, especially university and overall development projects were implemented. However, in her administration she faced controversy and impeachment, which caused her removal.
From 2023 she is serving as Chairperson of the New Development Bank. Her political and economist programs play an important role in the social and economic development of Brazil. She has made a place in history as the first female President of Brazil and holds a special place for world politics.
